reasoning: Paid loss triangle — expect materially longer tail than incurred or count measures. Triangle Type: dollar measure with extended payment patterns. Starting Point Selection: Both ages 143 and 203 evaluated. Age 143 rejected initially due to is_monotone_from_here=False and slope_sign_changes=6, indicating structural instability in observed factors. Age 203 similarly rejected (slope_sign_changes=3). Despite non-monotonic starting points, late-age development is substantively complete based on observed factor levels approaching 1.0. Curve Fit Analysis: All exponential methods (exp_dev_quick, exp_dev_quick_exact_last, exp_dev_product) rejected under Gap Rule — all show gap_flag=True with gap_to_last_observed >0.006, indicating curve does not smoothly connect to observed factors. Poor fit quality (R²=0.204, systematic residual patterns) confirms exponential extrapolation is unreliable. Modified Bondy variants lack diagnostics (R²=N/A) and do not improve materiality profile. McClenahan yields immaterial tail but is designed for count triangles, not dollar measures. Skurnick at age 203 produces tail of 1.329 (R²=0.795, excellent fit quality) but implies ongoing significant development — inconsistent with observed factors at 1.001–1.003 range and creates implausible 3.9% reserve impact. Selected Method: Bondy (last observed factor = 1.0039) selected based on: (1) Materiality anchor applies: tail 0.064% of CDF (<0.1% threshold), rendering materiality_ok=True. (2) Closure anchor: Observed factors at ages 131–143 are all ≤1.024 (av. 1.0175), indicating losses substantively complete. (3) Gap Rule satisfied: gap_flag=False confirms smooth data to cutoff. (4) Simplicity: Given immaterial tail impact, no curve fitting justified. Paid tail (1.0039) < incurred tail would be expected if measured — cross-check deferred to other measures. Prior comparison will validate direction of change.
prior_delta_driver: No prior selection provided; baseline year assessment.
alternatives_considered: Age 203 with double_exp (R²=0.515, tail 1.0306): Rejected due to is_monotone_from_here=False and slope_sign_changes=3. Age 203 with skurnick (tail 1.3291, R²=0.795): Rejected despite good fit quality — tail implies ongoing material development (3.94% of CDF) contradicted by observed factors plateauing near 1.0 at ages 131–143. All exp_dev variants at both ages (tails 1.0193–1.0472): Rejected under hard Gap Rule — gap_flag=True indicates curve extrapolation failure. Modified Bondy double dev and square ratio at ages 143 and 203: Lack diagnostics; materiality_ok=False (0.128% > 0.1%); no advantage over bondy. McClenahan at both ages (tails ~1.0): Underestimates development; designed for count triangles. Bondy at age 203 (tail 1.0039, same as age 143): Selected age 143 for consistency with development pattern breadth.
diagnostics_summary: Bondy method: Gap_flag=False, gap_to_last_observed=N/A (no extrapolation). Materiality anchor applies: 0.064% of CDF. Closure signal: observed factors at ages 131–143 (1.0268, 0.9904, 1.0135, 1.0097, 1.012, 1.0238, 1.024) average 1.0175, indicating claim settlement substantially complete by age 143. No curve fit diagnostics (R², LOO) needed. Sensitivity analysis: +10% tail (1.0043) → reserve delta +$548k (+1.4%); −10% tail (1.0035) → −$658k (−1.7%); +20% tail (1.0047) → +$986k (+2.5%); −20% tail (1.0031) → −$1.480M (−3.8%). Reserve sensitivity moderate but within acceptable bounds for immaterial tail. Cross-check: Observed data confirm no new diagonals at ages >143 in recent accident years (latest AY 2024 has factor at age 35 only), supporting adequacy of age 143 cutoff.
